features.details: Examine the homepage HTML structure and CSS to identify the current layout implementation. Look for fixed-width containers, max-width constraints, or grid/flex configurations that may be causing the right-side whitespace on large screens. Document the current breakpoints and container widths.
features.findings: Investigation completed. The main container utilizes a fixed max-width of 1200px defined in src/assets/styles/main.css. No media queries exist for screens > 1200px. Flexbox is used for internal alignment but the parent container width is the bottleneck.

features.description: Identify CSS rules causing whitespace issue
features.details: Pinpoint the specific CSS rules responsible for the layout constraint. This could be: fixed max-width on main containers, improper flexbox/grid configuration, or missing responsive breakpoints for large screens (typically >1440px or >1920px). Use browser DevTools to test modifications in real-time.

features.description: Implement responsive width adjustments for large screens
features.details: Modify the CSS to implement adaptive width for large screens. Options include: 1) Adding new media queries for large screens (min-width: 1440px, 1920px), 2) Using percentage-based widths with max-width constraints, 3) Implementing CSS Grid or Flexbox with proper justification, 4) Using CSS clamp() or min() for fluid sizing. Ensure content remains readable and properly aligned.

features.description: Adjust content containers and grid layouts
features.details: If the homepage uses a grid or card-based layout, adjust the number of columns or their widths for large screens. Consider using CSS Grid auto-fit/auto-fill with minmax() for automatic responsive column adjustment. Ensure cards, sidebars, and main content areas scale appropriately.

features.description: Test responsive layout across multiple screen sizes
features.details: Verify the layout works correctly across various screen sizes: mobile (<768px), tablet (768px-1024px), desktop (1024px-1440px), large desktop (1440px-1920px), and extra large (>1920px). Use browser DevTools responsive mode and test on actual devices if possible. Document any issues found.

features.description: Add visual regression tests for responsive layout
features.details: Create or update visual regression tests or snapshot tests to capture the homepage layout at different viewport sizes. This ensures future changes don't inadvertently break the responsive behavior. Use tools like Percy, Chromatic, or Playwright visual testing.

overall_approach: 1) Investigate the current homepage layout structure and CSS to identify the root cause of the fixed-width or constrained layout. 2) Implement responsive design techniques such as CSS media queries, flexible containers (flexbox/grid), or fluid width layouts to make the homepage adapt to larger screen sizes. 3) Test across multiple screen sizes to ensure the fix works without breaking smaller screen layouts.
potential_risks: Breaking the layout on smaller screens (tablets, mobile) while fixing large screens
Content overflow or alignment issues when expanding width
Images and media elements not scaling properly with the new layout
Text readability issues with wider content areas (lines too long to read comfortably)
Existing responsive breakpoints may conflict with new changes
